chunk_summary: This section outlines an age-staged feeding prescription template for infants and young children (0–24 months) during routine clinic visits, focusing on standardized counseling to improve feeding practices and measurable health outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of an age-staged feeding prescription for infants and young children.
Learn how to integrate feeding prescriptions into routine clinic visits to standardize counseling.
Identify key nutritional and supplementation guidelines for children aged 0–24 months.
Recognize the importance of follow-up plans and linking families to community support services.
key_facts: Feeding prescriptions should include five components: milk feeding plan, complementary foods, texture progression, supplementation schedule, and follow-up plan.
Vitamin D supplementation is recommended at 400 IU daily, with iron supplementation as per protocol.
Deworming should begin at 12 months, following local program guidelines.
For a 7-month-old breastfed infant with slowing weight gain: give two thick meals daily, advancing to half a katori per meal, enriched with ghee/oil.
Counseling should link to measurable outcomes such as weight gain, MMF (minimum meal frequency), MDD (minimum dietary diversity), and anemia prevention.
reference_quote: Guiding principles for feeding non-breastfed children 6–24 months of age — World Health Organization — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK143674/
document_key_topics: Standardized counseling during routine clinic visits
Follow-up plans and community support linkage
